---
title: "Untitled"
format: html
server: shiny
---

In this quarto document we call the "sim_functions_qtofneg.R" file code to use 
functions for similarity calculation between QTOF data and the Ftms assembled 
negative database.


```{r}
library(Spectra)
library(CompoundDb)
setwd("C:/Users/amentag/Desktop/these/These/clones/RDynlib/RDynLib/Similarity")
pth <- getwd()
pth
```


First we load the "sim_functions.R" file that contains functions for similarity 
calculation.
```{r}
source("sim_functions_qtof_ftms.R")
```

Here we install the new CompoundDb version
```{r}
#BiocManager::install("RforMassSpectrometry/CompoundDb", ref = "jomain", force = TRUE)
```



```{r}
load("data/ftms_assembled.RData")
ftms_MS2 <- ftms_assembled
spectraData(ftms_MS2)
```
Summary and quantile of the numbers of peaks for each spectrum in ftms data
```{r}
peak_counts_ftms <- lengths(ftms_MS2)
summary(peak_counts_ftms)
```

```{r}
quantile(peak_counts_ftms)
```


The number of spectra with just one peak
```{r}

num_one_peak_spectra_ftms <- sum(peak_counts_ftms == 1)
num_one_peak_spectra_ftms
```

138 spectrum with one peak 

Here we load the QTOF of flax seed database (it's already a spectra object)  
```{r}
load("data/ms2_qtof_sep.RData")
qtof_neg <- ms2_qtof_ftdda
spectraData(qtof_neg)
```
```{r}
peak_counts_qtof <- lengths(qtof_neg)
summary(peak_counts_qtof)
```

```{r}
quantile(peak_counts_qtof)
```

```{r}
num_one_peak_spectra_qtof <- sum(peak_counts_qtof == 1)
num_one_peak_spectra_qtof
```


Next we calculate the similarity score between the qtof and flax seed QTOF
negative integuments.

```{r}
#Calculate the similarity between each qtof collision energy and the ftms assembled data
res_dda1 <- sim_final_final(qtof_neg, ftms_MS2, collision_energy_qtof = "DDA1", threshold = 0.8)
res_dda2 <- sim_final_final(qtof_neg, ftms_MS2, collision_energy_qtof = "DDA2", threshold = 0.8)
res_dda3 <- sim_final_final(qtof_neg, ftms_MS2, collision_energy_qtof = "DDA3", threshold = 0.8)

#Combine all the similarity results into one dataframe
DDA_combined <- rbind(result_DDA1, result_DDA2, result_DDA3)
#save the result to a .RData object
save(DDA_combined, file = "data/DDA1_DDA2_DDA3.RData")


res_dda1 
res_dda2
res_dda3
```

The list of features that hit for each collision energy

```{r}
hits_dda1 <- unique(res_dda1$target_feature_id)
hits_dda2 <- unique(res_dda2$target_feature_id)
hits_dda3 <- unique(res_dda3$target_feature_id)

hits_dda1
hits_dda2
hits_dda3

```

The list of features that overlap for each collision energy
```{r}
overlap_12 <- intersect(hits_dda1, hits_dda2)
overlap_13 <- intersect(hits_dda1, hits_dda3)
overlap_23 <- intersect(hits_dda2, hits_dda3)
overlap_all <- Reduce(intersect, list(hits_dda1, hits_dda2, hits_dda3))

overlap_12
overlap_13
overlap_23
overlap_all

```



```{r}
cols <- c("rtime", "precursorMz", "original_precursor_mz",
          "feature_rtmed", "feature_id", "dda",
          "target_rtime", "target_precursorMz", "target_peaksCount",
          "target_feature_rtmed", "target_feature_id",
          "score", "matched_peaks_count")
DDA_combined <- DDA_combined[order(DDA_combined$feature_id), ] |>
    as.data.frame()
```

We next summarize the results for the query features, listing the different
target feature IDs.

```{r}
tmp <- split(DDA_combined[, c("feature_id", "dda", "target_feature_id",
                              "feature_rtmed", "target_feature_rtmed",
                              "matched_peaks_count")],
             DDA_combined$feature_id)
#' Get the matches with a 1:1 matching for features
one <- vapply(tmp, function(z) length(unique(z$target_feature_id)) == 1L, NA)


```

The table below lists the matches with a 1:1 match between features in QTOF and
FTMS.

```{r, results = "asis"}
tab <- do.call(rbind, tmp[one])
rownames(tab) <- NULL
pandoc.table(tab[, c("feature_id", "dda", "target_feature_id",
                     "matched_peaks_count")],
             style = "rmarkdown",
             caption = paste0("Matches between QTOF and FTMS fragment ",
                              "spectra with a 1:1 matching of features."))
```

For some features only a single QTOF MS2 spectrum matches, while for others all
QTOF MS2 spectra from the different collision energies match. Note however that
the number of matched peaks is very low.

We next evaluate the hits where QTOF MS2 spectra match multiple or different
FTMS fragment spectra.

```{r, results = "asis"}
tab <- do.call(rbind, tmp[!one])
rownames(tab) <- NULL
pandoc.table(tab[, c("feature_id", "dda", "target_feature_id",
                     "target_feature_rtmed", "matched_peaks_count")],
             style = "rmarkdown",
             caption = paste0("Matches between QTOF and FTMS fragment ",
                              "spectra with a 1:n matching of features."),
             split.table = Inf)

```


The number of matched peaks is in most cases larger than 1. The feature IDs of
FTMS fragment spectra with multi matches are similar, in most cases consecutive
numbers suggesting that these features are co-eluting, which is can also be seen
by their retention time.

Next we also compare the retention times of the features with matches.

```{r}
plot(3, 3, pch = NA, ylim = c(1, 4), yaxt = "n", ylab = "", xlab = "rtime",
     xlim = range(DDA_combined[, c("feature_rtmed", "target_feature_rtmed")]))
grid()
cols <- rep("#ff000080", nrow(DDA_combined))
cols[DDA_combined$dda == "DDA2"] <- "#00ff0080"
cols[DDA_combined$dda == "DDA3"] <- "#0000ff80"
for (i in seq_len(nrow(DDA_combined))) {
    lines(x = c(DDA_combined$feature_rtmed[i],
                DDA_combined$target_feature_rtmed[i]),
          y = c(1, 4), type = "b", col = cols[i])
}
axis(side = 2, at = c(1, 4), label = c("QTOF", "FTMS"))
legend("bottomright", col = c("#ff000080", "#00ff0080", "#0000ff80"),
       legend = c("DDA1", "DDA2", "DDA3"), pch = 1)
```

There are some problematic matches, i.e., QTOF MS2 spectra that match multiple
FTMS spectra with different retention times. On the other hand, we could use
this information to clean the matches and remove potentially wrong assignments.
